title: agent-dep-types: four-kind dep edges + fixpoint ready cascade
body:
# Goal Extend the dependency edge model from one kind (`blocks`) to four kinds (`blocks`, `parent-child`, `related`, `discovered-from`). Add CLI verbs to manage dep edges explicitly (`jjf dep add|rm|tree`). Make `jjf ready` honor the new edge semantics. End state: `jjf dep add --kind parent-child` writes a typed edge; `jjf ready` excludes children of in-flight parents; `jjf dep tree ` prints the dependency graph rooted at ``. # Why now Today jjforge ships one dep kind: an opaque "blocks" edge stored as `dependencies: Vec` on `IssueRecord`. We've already hit the gap: epics carry children via the `epic:` label, not via the dep graph â€” so `jjf ready` can't reason about epic membership. beads (per `reference/beads/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:193-201`) ships four kinds with different semantics: - `blocks`: hard prereq. Blocked until cleared. - `parent-child`: hierarchical (epic â†” child ticket). Children inherit parent's blocked state via fixpoint. - `related`: soft cross-link. Reference only; no ready effect. - `discovered-from`: "I found this issue while working on X." Provenance; no ready effect. Adopting this gives us a real epic-membership graph (no more label-only encoding), distinguishes "blocks me" from "interesting cross-reference," and tracks subagent follow-ups by provenance. # Scope ## Storage ### Edge schema bump - Today: `dependencies: Vec`. - Tomorrow: `dependencies: Vec` where: ```rust pub struct DepEdge { pub target: IssueId, pub kind: DepKind, } pub enum DepKind { Blocks, ParentChild, // self is child OF target Related, DiscoveredFrom, } ``` - Serde: tagged as `{"target": "...", "kind": "blocks"}` in the JSON record. - v1 records (untyped `dependencies`) read as `Vec` for backward compat. - Spec bump: v2.2 or fold into next bump. ### New ops - `Op::AddDepEdge { issue_id, target, kind }`. - `Op::RemoveDepEdge { issue_id, target, kind }`. - Existing `Op::AddDep { issue_id, target }` becomes a v1-only alias that reads as `AddDepEdge { ..., kind: Blocks }`. ### Storage API ```rust impl Storage { pub fn add_dep_edge(&self, issue_id: &IssueId, target: &IssueId, kind: DepKind) -> Result<()>; pub fn remove_dep_edge(&self, issue_id: &IssueId, target: &IssueId, kind: DepKind) -> Result<()>; pub fn dep_tree(&self, root: &IssueId) -> Result; } ``` ### `Storage::list_ready` semantics under the new model Reuse beads' fixpoint algorithm (see `reference/beads/internal/storage/issueops/blocked_consistency.go`): - An issue X is blocked if any `blocks`-kind dep edge points to an open OR in-progress issue. - An issue X is also blocked if X has a `parent-child` edge where target is open AND target itself is blocked. (Closed parents don't block children; the cascade follows the parent's actual blocked-ness, not parent's status alone.) - `related` and `discovered-from` edges never affect blocked computation. - One-pass fixpoint: compute blocked-ness iteratively until no changes. v1 acceptable: read-all, single cascade pass per call (still a small graph). ## CLI - `jjf dep add ` â€” default `--kind blocks`. - `jjf dep add --kind ` â€” explicit kind. `` âˆˆ {blocks, parent-child, related, discovered-from}. - `jjf dep rm --kind ` â€” remove edge. - `jjf dep tree ` â€” print rooted tree (text or `--json`). - `jjf new -d ` (existing) keeps adding `blocks` edges by default; new `--dep :` syntax allows other kinds inline: ``` jjf new --dep blocks:abc1234 --dep discovered-from:def5678 ``` - `jjf show ` plain-text section adds: ``` dependencies: blocks: , parent-child (parent): related: discovered-from: ``` - `--json` envelope: `dependencies` becomes the new tagged array. ## Spec - `docs/storage-format.md`: - Â§3 IssueRecord: `dependencies` field shape change. - Â§3.x: enum `DepKind` + ready semantics rules. - Â§5.2 op vocab: add-dep-edge, remove-dep-edge. - `docs/cli-json.md`: - `dep` subcommand family. - Updated `new` / `show` envelopes for the tagged dependencies field. ## Migration Existing 17 tickets: dep field today is `Vec`, read path materializes it as `Vec`. No backfill needed â€” the read path handles v1-shape records transparently. # Acceptance - [ ] `jjf dep add

--kind blocks` writes a blocks edge; `jjf show ` displays it. - [ ] All four kinds parse and round-trip. - [ ] `jjf dep rm` removes the named kind only. - [ ] `jjf ready` excludes children of blocked parents via `parent-child` cascade. - [ ] `jjf ready` ignores `related` / `discovered-from` edges. - [ ] `jjf dep tree ` prints the parent-child tree. - [ ] v1 records (no kind tag) read as `Blocks`. - [ ] `jjf push` / `jjf pull` round-trips the new field shape. - [ ] Storage tests cover each kind through the fixpoint. - [ ] CLI integration tests for each `dep` verb. - [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green. # Out of scope - Replacing the `epic:` label with parent-child edges. Both can coexist; migration is a separate ticket. - Cycle detection on add. v1 trusts the operator. - A graph-visualization output (`jjf dep tree --dot`). Plain-text tree only. - Beads' `bd dep` subcommands beyond `add` / `rm` / `tree` (they ship more â€” `dep search`, `dep stats`). - Hierarchical IDs (beads' `bd-abc.1.2`). Our 7-hex ids stay flat. # Pointers - Reference impl: `reference/beads/internal/storage/dependency_queries.go` and `reference/beads/internal/storage/issueops/blocked_consistency.go` (fixpoint). - Edge kind taxonomy: `reference/beads/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:193-201`. - Storage precedent: existing `dependencies` field in `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs`. - Op precedent: `Op::AddDep` / `Op::RemoveDep` in `crates/jjf-storage/src/op.rs`. - `list_ready` to extend: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`.
